opp.name: FGV Holdings Berhad
opp.next_step: 50% confidence 1st June- Delays continue- aim to connect exec with ck by mid June FGV and FELDA merger is stretching - and this is delaying the process Evaluation taking time as they are restructuring their senior team. For now the visibility is third quarter RFP shortlist to be announced after Hari Raya - April 23rd Feb - RFP submitted 29th Jan- New RFP received- need to requalify and speak to prospect asap 5th Sept 2025- No project- deal omitted 16th Aug- Transition ck- KPMG well placed here- ck to check with KPMG RFP likely in 2024 (Viraj as contacts and some relationships) 19th June - Solution presentation schedule on 15/7. KPMG will join the session as they are the preferred partner for FGV. 14th June - Will meet up with CPO on 14th June @ 6.00p.m. Will provide more update after the meeting. 30th May - Meet up with Procurement, already schedule 2nd meeting with CPO on 14th June for S2P discussion. SAP Is our main competitor in this account. 2ndMay - Meeting with Procurement and IT team confirm on 16/5. 19th April - Will work with KPMG on this account as KPMG have good connection and can bring value to us. Awaiting for KPMG to schedule a meeting with FGV by end of April for further discussion. 21st March : > BD Spoke to Siti Balquish (Head, Supplier Contract & Relationship Management, Group Procurement) > They are currently put the project on hold as they are having difficulty getting other users and departments to give the green light for this solution 14th March : BD to follow up with POC and Adelene will be working with KPMG to sync up 28th Feb - Schedule meeting with FGV together with KPMG on 7/3. 1st Feb: BD trying to get updates on timelines for RFP 13th December : BD received information from Head of Category Procurement that they are likely to commence RFP in Feb 9th November : Response received by BD : They are still in the midst of the evaluation stage. They will let us know once we finalize and update you for the next action. 26th October : Follow ups done with POCs. As Nur Zumaira Harun is on leave till tomorrow, she will revert by Monday 28th August Nur Zumaira Harun (Group Procurement Team) have come back asking for a functional specification on Zycus Contract Management from us. Looped in Adelene and Nitish to communicate further on the same Indicative quote for budgeting purpose- to Siti
opp.ais_why:
• Across 2,205 chars of NextStep covering ~14 dated entries from Aug 2023 through Apr 2026 ‚Äî including KPMG partnership, RFP submission, multiple senior-team restructures, SAP competition ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI despite Merlin being in product scope. Per the 23 Feb 2026 entry: 'RFP submitted.' Per Apr 2026: 'RFP shortlist to be announced after Hari Raya - April.'
• On 21 Mar 2025: 'BD Spoke to Siti Balquish (Head, Supplier Contract & Relationship Management, Group Procurement). They are currently put the project on hold as they are having difficulty getting other users and departments to give the green light for this solution.' On 30 May 2024: 'Meet up with Procurement, already schedule 2nd meeting with CPO on 14th June for S2P discussion. SAP Is our main competitor in this account.' Long-running SAP-vs-Zycus comparison driven by KPMG partner involvement ‚Äî no AI evaluation dimension.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually long timespan (~32 months) and consistent traditional-S2P framing. Merlin is bundled but absent from any buyer dialogue. Score 3 (AI Resistant) reflects a long-running KPMG-influenced SAP-competitive deal with zero AI signal across 14+ touchpoints.

items.area: Post-merger decision timeline and process owner
items.quote: Evaluation taking time as they are restructuring their senior team; visibility third quarter

sources: Next_Step__c 2024-03-21 (Siti Balquish Sanudin, departmental buy-in difficulty)
metrics.narrative: FGV articulated the business problem and operational scope across the Next Step history: a group-wide Source-to-Pay modernization for Group Procurement covering a large supplier base and high RFx volume, with the initiative driving CPO-level S2P discussions (Next_Step__c, 2024-05-30 CPO meeting scheduled 2024-06-14). The buyer's own framing of the core pain was 'difficulty getting other users and departments to give the green light for this solution' (Next_Step__c, 2024-03-21, Siti Balquish Sanudin during a BD conversation). No ROI, savings, or cost figure was ever quantified by the buyer, so the value case remains a qualitative efficiency-and-scale narrative rather than a financial one. Metrics are partial: the problem and operational scale are documented, but a quantified business case is missing and must be built in an ROI workshop if the deal re-engages.

champion.narrative: No strong, active champion is identified. Siti Balquish Sanudin (Head, Supplier Contract & Relationship Management, Group Procurement; Influencer per OpportunityContactRole) was the early BD contact (2024-03-21) and shared internal intel, but she also reported the project was put on hold due to departmental resistance, signalling she lacked the authority to drive consensus, and she has had no documented contact since. Mac Chew (Head of Group Procurement, Decision Maker) is mapped but shows no championing behavior; his only recent touch was declining a July event invitation (Task, 2026-06-15). KPMG is the preferred partner (Next_Step__c, 2024-06-19) and a potential external coach, but its current advocacy is unconfirmed. The merger has likely sidelined all internal sponsors. Building a real champion with post-merger authority is the prerequisite to any forward motion.

competition.narrative: SAP is the primary competitor and incumbent: Next_Step__c 2024-05-30 explicitly states 'SAP Is our main competitor in this account', and the account runs an SAP ERP footing, giving SAP a unified-stack integration advantage. Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and iValua are also named in the Competitor field, so this is a multi-vendor RFP. Zycus's wedge is best-of-breed S2P depth (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, iRisk) plus KPMG as integration partner; the buyer's explicit interest in Zycus Contract Management (functional-spec request, 2024-08-28) is a concrete differentiation foothold versus SAP. The problem is activation: no competitive review, SAP-displacement narrative, or integration/ROI positioning has been documented since the RFP was submitted 130 days ago, so SAP can advance by default while Zycus is static.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and buyer-stated: FGV needs to modernize a group-wide Source-to-Pay process, and the acute organizational pain is internal alignment, quoted verbatim as 'difficulty getting other users and departments to give the green light for this solution' (Next_Step__c, 2024-03-21, Siti Balquish Sanudin, Head of Supplier Contract & Relationship Management). This is an adoption/consensus pain owned inside Group Procurement, compounded now by the merger and restructuring that have frozen decision-making. The operational pain (managing a large supplier base and high RFx volume on a legacy/SAP footing) is implied by the product scope and SAP-competitive framing. Pain is real but its owner keeps changing as the org restructures.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are documented: there is no MSA/SOW status, legal or procurement channel, InfoSec review, or contract-expiration forcing function in the record. KPMG is the named implementation partner (Next_Step__c, 2024-06-19), which will shape the paper and implementation scope once selection occurs, but no scoping is formalized. Given the deal has not cleared vendor shortlisting, paper process is genuinely early and unknown; the step that would create visibility is securing the RFP shortlist outcome and a mutual evaluation-to-contract timeline from the buyer.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified anywhere: Contact Roles name a Decision Maker (Mac Chew, Head of Group Procurement), Evaluators, and Influencers, but no one is tagged Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor, and no budget-authority field is populated. The Next Step history references a CPO (S2P discussion scheduled 2024-06-14) but never names that person, so the budget owner above Group Procurement is unmapped. The FGV-FELDA merger and senior-team restructuring (Next_Step__c, 2026-06-01) mean the budget-authority chain is actively changing, which raises the risk that any previously-assumed sponsor is no longer in seat. Identifying and reaching the post-merger budget owner (likely a CPO or CFO above Mac Chew) is the single biggest structural gap and a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, multi-vendor RFP process out of Group Procurement, with KPMG as the preferred implementation partner joining evaluation sessions (Next_Step__c, 2024-06-19). The process has been repeatedly stalled by internal factors: departmental resistance to green-lighting the solution (2024-03-21), a project hold, and most recently the FGV-FELDA merger and senior-team restructuring that pushed visibility to the third quarter and delayed the shortlist announcement past its April target (Next_Step__c, 2026-06-01). The approval chain beyond Group Procurement is undocumented, and the merger is redrawing who signs off. The process is understood at a high level but its current owner, timeline, and approval gates are frozen and unclear.

sources: Next_Step__c 2024-08-28 (functional spec request, Nur Zumaira Harun)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is running as a formal RFP (submitted 2026-02-23), so structured decision criteria exist inside that RFP, but the specific weighted criteria are not documented in Salesforce and no buyer call captures them. What the history does show is that Contract Management is a criterion of interest: Nur Zumaira Harun (Group Procurement) requested a functional specification on Zycus Contract Management (Next_Step__c, 2024-08-28), pointing to iContract as an area the buyer is actively scrutinizing. Integration with the incumbent SAP ERP environment is an implicit criterion given SAP is the main competitor. Criteria are partial: the RFP frames them but they are not reconstructed in the record, and recovering the RFP scoring rubric from the buyer is a needed step.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — SAP is the single strongest current threat: the incumbent ERP vendor and explicitly named 'main competitor' (Next_Step__c, 2024-05-30), advancing by default in a merger-frozen process where Zycus has run no displacement play in 130 days. Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and iValua are named in the RFP field as active alternatives but carry no buyer-preference or negative signal, so they rank as medium/low. There is no evidence any rival has been eliminated. The wedge against SAP is best-of-breed depth plus buyer-validated interest in Zycus Contract Management (functional-spec request, 2024-08-28) and Merlin, activated through KPMG.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: FGV Holdings Berhad — Formal Evaluation · $400K · closes 20 Nov

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 23 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 52; business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 7 is anchored on the stage win (23), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement on the deal is dark: no two-way deal dialogue in 60+ days, the last touch was a declined event invitation, and the RFP shortlist is long overdue amid a merger freeze.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2023-04-18, now ~1172 days old. In Formal Evaluation since 2026-02-26 stage change, but the account has been evaluating S2P for 40+ months with no forward stage progression documented. Close date 2026-11-20 is 140 days out. RFP submitted 2026-02-23 (130 days ago); shortlist expected post-Hari Raya April 2026, now ~10 weeks overdue. CloseDate has slipped three times in 2026 (2026-06-20 to 2026-09-20 to 2026-11-20). No buyer-facing deliverable executed and no two-way buyer deal-engagement in the last 60 days: the 2026-06-15 activity is a rep-side event invitation that Mac Chew declined. Required pace to hit 2026-11-20 (shortlist decision, vendor selection, commercials, negotiation, contracting) is not credible while the merger freeze holds. Cold 60+ days with no buyer engagement forces Off Track.
evidence: FGV-FELDA merger stretching the evaluation and delaying visibility to third quarter (Next_Step__c, 2026-06-01)
RFP shortlist announcement expected post-Hari Raya April 2026, now ~10 weeks overdue (Next_Step__c)

CloseDate slipped three times in 2026: 2026-06-20 to 2026-09-20 (2026-05-03) to 2026-11-20 (2026-05-11) (OpportunityFieldHistory)
SAP stated as main competitor and incumbent (Next_Step__c, 2024-05-30); no competitive review or displacement strategy documented since RFP submission 2026-02-23
No buyer call recordings exist for this opportunity across opp, account, and attendee-email discovery
Deal age ~1172 days with no stage advancement despite RFP submission
north_star_verdict.headline: 140 days to close, in Formal Evaluation for 40+ months since 2023-04-18. FGV-FELDA merger and senior-team restructuring have frozen progress since RFP submission 2026-02-23 (130 days ago); the shortlist expected post-Hari Raya (April) is now ~10 weeks overdue. No Economic Buyer identified. Decision Maker Mac Chew is mapped but has no documented deal engagement. SAP is the incumbent and primary competitor with no displacement strategy in 130 days. The only recent buyer touch (2026-06-15) was Mac Chew declining a July event invitation, not deal progression.

flags: SAP is the incumbent and named main competitor but no displacement play has run in 130 days: build an SAP-ECC integration and Merlin wedge with KPMG before the shortlist lands.
The account is single-threaded into Group Procurement with no economic buyer mapped and a merger reshuffling leadership: re-map the post-merger budget owner (CPO/CFO) above Mac Chew.
There is zero buyer call evidence on this deal ever: secure a direct buyer working session to recover live signal and reconfirm the evaluation timeline.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a merger-frozen, SAP-incumbent RFP with no economic buyer and no live buyer dialogue. The three highest-leverage levers are to restart a real buyer conversation, activate an SAP-displacement play, and re-map power after the restructuring.

competitive_position.summary: SAP is the single strongest current threat: the incumbent ERP vendor and explicitly named 'main competitor' (Next_Step__c, 2024-05-30), advancing by default in a merger-frozen process where Zycus has run no displacement play in 130 days. Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and iValua are named in the RFP field as active alternatives but carry no buyer-preference or negative signal, so they rank as medium/low. There is no evidence any rival has been eliminated. The wedge against SAP is best-of-breed depth plus buyer-validated interest in Zycus Contract Management (functional-spec request, 2024-08-28) and Merlin, activated through KPMG.

competitors.name: SAP
competitors.quote: SAP Is our main competitor in this account
competitors.how_we_win: Position best-of-breed S2P depth plus a documented SAP-ECC integration roadmap with KPMG, and make Zycus Contract Management the differentiation wedge the buyer already asked about.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin AI sourcing and unified iContract-to-eInvoicing suite versus GEP's services-led model.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast total cost and contract-management depth against Coupa; lead with Merlin agentic sourcing.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize faster time-to-value and stronger contract lifecycle management than Ivalua for this supplier scale.
